meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve no version control (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Debugging with AI for Version Control Issues
content.seo_description: Master AI-driven debugging to overcome version control gaps. A professional guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to secure your codebase.
content.answer_block: Without version control, a single breaking change is catastrophic. You can mitigate this by using AI to generate comprehensive state-snapshots and automated diff-logs. By integrating Antigravity for systematic code analysis and Claude Code for iterative repairs, you transform a fragile, unversioned folder into a resilient, production-ready development environment.
content.intro:
You are staring at a directory of unversioned files—a ticking time bomb where one wrong keystroke could wipe out weeks of progress. The anxiety of building without a safety net is the silent killer of student innovation. You aren't just coding; you are balancing on a tightrope without a harness.

But the era of manual, error-prone recovery is over. By leveraging the advanced reasoning of Claude Code and the surgical precision of Antigravity, you can turn your chaotic workspace into a versioned, self-documenting ecosystem. It is time to stop fearing your own code and start mastering the architecture of recovery.

steps.title: Establish a Baseline State
steps.description: Before changing anything, use AI to map the current architecture of your project to understand dependencies and logic flows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this directory and generate a high-level architectural map. Identify all critical entry points and dependency chains that are currently at risk due to lack of versioning.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'map project structure and identify high-risk modules'

steps.title: Generate AI-Managed Change Logs
steps.description: Since you lack Git history, create a synthetic history by forcing the AI to generate detailed diff summaries for every session.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a 'change_log.md' format that captures the intention, the specific code block modified, and the expected side effects of the current session.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a change_log.md file and document current state'

steps.title: Automated Bug Isolation
steps.description: Isolate regressions by comparing your current file state against the documented baseline established in step one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare the current file state against the baseline. Isolate the specific function or module causing the recent regression.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'find the regression point by comparing current state to baseline'

steps.title: Implement Synthetic Rollback Points
steps.description: Create 'emergency restore' files that capture the state of your core logic before performing high-risk refactoring.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a backup function that copies critical files into a /recovery folder with a timestamped metadata header.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'execute backup of core logic to /recovery directory'

steps.title: Refactor with Verification Loops
steps.description: Use Claude Code to verify that the fix addresses the bug without introducing new technical debt or logical conflicts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the proposed fix for logical consistency and potential side effects. Ensure no new dependencies are introduced.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'verify code fix against baseline constraints'

pro_tips: Always treat your 'change_log.md' as the single source of truth; if it's not in the log, it didn't happen.
Use Antigravity to generate unit tests based on your current code; these tests act as a safety net when you lack traditional Git branches.
Keep your /recovery folder outside your primary workspace to prevent accidental deletion during bulk cleanups.
Force Claude Code to explain its reasoning before applying changes to ensure you understand the 'why' behind the fix.
Periodically move your project into a proper Git repository once you reach a 'stable milestone' to graduate from manual recovery.
content.next_steps: Once you have stabilized your code, transition this project to a Git repository and push it to GitHub to build your professional portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools to turn your unversioned mess into a professional-grade codebase. Don't let the fear of losing progress stall your career growth. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
